home.heroTitle: ResQLink – Sri Lanka's Intelligent Disaster Response Platform
home.heroSubtitle: Connecting citizens, volunteers, and authorities across Sri Lanka to report incidents, coordinate relief efforts, and respond to floods, landslides, and coastal emergencies in real time.
home.featuresSubtitle: Purpose-built tools for Sri Lanka's disaster response ecosystem
home.howSubtitle: A streamlined response workflow from incident to resolution
home.rolesSubtitle: Tailored experiences for every user in Sri Lanka's response network
home.ctaTitle: Protect Sri Lanka's Communities with ResQLink
home.ctaSubtitle: Join the platform trusted by DMC partners, NDRC volunteers, and district coordinators to coordinate relief and save lives during emergencies.
home.footerTagline: Sri Lanka's unified disaster response and coordination platform

features.incidentDesc: Citizens across Sri Lanka report floods, landslides, and storm damage with precise location, description, and media.
features.aiDesc: Intelligent risk assessment aligned with DMC and NBRO severity classification standards for accurate prioritisation.
features.volunteerDesc: Deploy NDRC volunteers and relief resources efficiently across Sri Lanka's 25 districts during active emergencies.
features.alertDesc: Real-time alerts from the Disaster Management Centre and NBRO delivered instantly to the right field responders.

steps.step1Desc: Citizens submit disaster details with location and media via the platform
steps.step2Desc: AI analyses impact and prioritises response in line with DMC guidelines
steps.step3Desc: NDRC volunteers and district resources are coordinated and assigned
steps.step4Desc: Real-time tracking and status updates until full resolution

rolesCitizen.title: Citizen
rolesCitizen.item1: Report flood, landslide, or storm incidents

rolesAdmin.title: Administrator
rolesAdmin.item1: Review and verify DMC incident reports
rolesAdmin.item2: Coordinate district resources and relief camps
